chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: So I was on a roll.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: I was an executive with a nice salary, annual bonuses and stock options, all the perks.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: Everything was on track.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: And on Monday, January 7th, 2008, at 3 o'clock in the afternoon in a small conference room

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: on the top floor of our building, the president of the company wanted to have a quick meeting

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: with me, which wasn't unusual since he was my boss.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: But the meeting turned out to be even more brief than expected.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: He fired me.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: And I'll never forget how his words just sucked the breath right out of me.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: And I left the conference room in a dazed state, and I went home and curled up my bed

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: in the fetal position for three hours.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: And while I could go on in vivid detail about how I felt, what I did to my self-esteem,

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: my finances and so on, what I now realize is while that event created the greatest amount

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: of discomfort I had ever felt, it was that discomfort, the departure from my ordered

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: life that forever changed it for the better.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: To see friends, what makes you comfortable can ruin you.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: And what makes you uncomfortable is the only way to grow.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: Let me say that again.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: What makes you comfortable can ruin you.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: And only in a state of discomfort can you continually grow.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: Now I suppose if on January 8th somebody come up and said, gee, buddy, getting fired is

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: a good thing because now you're really going to grow.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: I probably would have smacked them.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: But pretty quickly I became motivated to start a new journey.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: And after a couple of years of work with my new team and a PhD friend at the University

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: of Nebraska, we had this epiphany on how to illustrate and apply the science of discomfort

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: and growth.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: We called the concept the growth rings.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: The growth rings represent living environments that promote or hinder growth.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: And that includes everything from your place of work to even a fish bowl.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: You see what dictates the size of a goldfish is its environment.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: And while this goldfish lives in a very safe environment, it's also very limiting in most

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: every way.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: And when placed in a more robust environment, like say a small pond, this can be the result.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: Now it also means he could be eaten.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: But friends, this is you.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: The environments in which you work, live, and play, they're all a proverbial fish bowl

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: that dictates your growth.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: So the first growth ring represents a low performing, low growth environment called

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: stagnation.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: Stagnation is understood by having to follow too many steps and permissions of minutiae.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: Thus stifles creativity, independent thought, and action.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: To imagine an environment such as this, think no further than our state and federal governments.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: Now the antithesis of stagnation is chaos, also low growth and low performing.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: Chaos can be caused by internal or external events or conditions.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: We see chaos occur at times when business mergers, natural disasters, and horrific events

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: like 99.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: Chaos is having zero predictability or control over inputs and outcomes.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: Coming back down the growth rings, next to stagnation is the most desirable environment,

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: order.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: Order is knowing that what you do or what is happening in your environment leads to

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: a predictable outcome.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: And in predictability, comfort is found.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: But comfort is also what makes order so dangerous.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: Because science shows that any time you continually do something or even think about something

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: the same way, you'll eventually stop growing.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: And this applies to every living thing, even our dog.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: You see if Aspen had a chance, she'd choose comfort six days a week and twice on Sundays.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: But too much growth limiting order would have prevented her from becoming a therapy dog.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: And had this been allowed, think of the lives this gentle soul would not be touching today.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: So before your order continues to limit the way you think and act, remember what I said

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: earlier, growth only occurs in a state of discomfort.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: Now think about the power of that phrase, growth only occurs in a state of discomfort.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: I can unequivocally state I wouldn't be standing on this stage today without my uncomfortable

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: order disrupting day nine years ago.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: By the way, I'm not recommending you go get fired to see if it leads to a TED talk.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: But when you feel discomfort hit, that means you've entered the complexity ring.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: Complexity is nothing more than changed order.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: But when your order is changed, outcomes are no longer predictable.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: And it's unpredictability that makes you uncomfortable.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: And while most times your visceral response to discomfort is not just no but hell no,

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: you can actually learn how empowering it is to consciously acknowledge discomfort.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: And then when appropriate, choose complexity over order.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: And I know seeking discomfort sounds odd and not many people do it.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: But you have to learn to embrace it because it's the only environment where sustained

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: or exponential growth can occur.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: Okay, to weave high growth complexity into the fabric of your lives, there are three

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: primary ways it can be triggered.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: Complexity trigger number one is it can be forced upon you.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: When I got fired, I didn't have a chance to stay in order.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: Complexity was selected for me.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: And when this happens, how much you grow depends on how you respond to it.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: Now, I could have remained angry, I could have used it as an excuse.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: But what I actually learned is that I suck as an employee.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: And I'm much better off accepting the risks of running my own company.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: Complexity trigger number two, someone can help you get there.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: This is the role of parents, teachers, coaches, and bosses, because left on their own,

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: people will consciously or subconsciously select the comfort of order.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: And they then need to be pushed into complexity in order to continue growing.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: My youngest daughter spent most of her high school life training to play tennis.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: And her coach was pretty familiar with our work on complexity and the growth rings.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: So I called him up one day to check on Maddie's progress.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: And I was able to phrase my question like this.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: I said, hey, Lee, how long has been since Maddie's been pushed deep into complexity?

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: Lee's response, hmm, funny you'd ask, Bill.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: We got there yesterday.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: She broke down into tears on the tennis court.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: Huh.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: Well, knowing how tough my daughter is, and the fact that she never cries,

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: told me she was deep into complexity.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: But friends, this is where critical developmental decisions are made.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: Because the old Bill, the pre-growth ring Bill, would have intervened

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: and wanted to know what was making her so uncomfortable.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: Then I would have done everything I could to try and get her happy again.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: What I really would have been doing is removing the complexity

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: and putting her in order.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: I actually would have been stifling her development.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: But the new post-growth ring Bill relished in his daughter's discomfort.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: And it was the coach's next words that told me everything I needed to hear.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: He said, but Bill, I got to tell you, it took a heck of a lot more to get her to the limits of complexity this month than it did last month.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: Discomfort was causing her growth.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: Okay, but what if you're not lucky enough to live or work in a robust high-growth environment?

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: What if you're stuck in order, or even worse, stagnation?

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: Well, the great news is everyone can trigger complexity at any time.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: So complexity trigger number three, trigger it yourself.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: Take a journey with me back to Montgomery, Alabama in the 1950s.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: And imagine, if you will, a young girl who's black and she attends an all-black high school.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: And she takes a city bus to get there and home, which wasn't unusual in that era.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: And on March 2nd, 1955, she boards a bus to come home from school.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: And she sits near the back in the first row of seats where blacks were allowed to sit.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: And as the bus continues to fill with white people, there's eventually no more room in the front of the bus.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: And according to local law, she needed to move further back to create room for white people to sit.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: You see, Montgomery had an order in place that when followed led to a very predictable outcome.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: Repression of people with little conflict.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: But 15-year-old Claudette Colvin had just spent the last month in high school studying black history.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: And she was understandably fed up with the historic and existing atrocities.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: And so on this day, she decided she didn't like Montgomery's order.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: And by refusing to give up her seat, she sent a community, our law enforcement, to the front of the bus.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: And she was the first person to get to the front of the bus.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: And by refusing to give up her seat, she sent a community, our laws, and our entire country into complexity.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: Yes, nine months before Rosa Parks made her famous decision to stay put,

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: it was a 15-year-old girl that was handcuffed, dragged from the bus, and taken to prison.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: It was Ms. Colvin, not Rosa Parks, who first fought the law.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: And by the way, was also the star plaintiff to testify in the famous lawsuit that went all the way to the US Supreme Court.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: So I use Claudette's actions not to heighten awareness of race issues, although that's not necessarily bad.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: But I use it as an example of every issue, of every situation in an ordered environment.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: It's a real and perfect example of complexity forcing people, our communities, and our courts into discomfort.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: And the downstream impact that can occur any time someone elects to move from order.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: Dr. Serene Jones, in a recent book, summarizes this concept very eloquently.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: She said,

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: The constant facade of order hides the wilderness that is craving to seep out and teach us that life wasn't created to be what we think it is.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: Beyond words, we must experience the wilderness to be taught what cannot be otherwise known.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: So friends, it's not the discomfort of losing a job.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: It's not having a child breakdown on the tennis courts.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: But it's order you should fear the most because it is a threat.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: And order disrupting people like Jesus, Galileo, Claudette Colvin, Aspen's trainer, and maybe even a few of you have already proven.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: Now think about this.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: It's not the complexity triggering individuals or events you should fear the most.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: But it's your own willingness to accept or seek discomfort that will dictate the growth of not just you, but our entire world.

chunks.title: Why_comfort_will_ruin_your_life
chunks.text: Thank you very much for allowing me to be a part of this.

text: So I was on a roll. I was an executive with a nice salary, annual bonuses and stock options, all the perks. Everything was on track. And on Monday, January 7th, 2008, at 3 o'clock in the afternoon in a small conference room on the top floor of our building, the president of the company wanted to have a quick meeting with me, which wasn't unusual since he was my boss. But the meeting turned out to be even more brief than expected. He fired me. And I'll never forget how his words just sucked the breath right out of me. And I left the conference room in a dazed state, and I went home and curled up my bed in the fetal position for three hours. And while I could go on in vivid detail about how I felt, what I did to my self-esteem, my finances and so on, what I now realize is while that event created the greatest amount of discomfort I had ever felt, it was that discomfort, the departure from my ordered life that forever changed it for the better. To see friends, what makes you comfortable can ruin you. And what makes you uncomfortable is the only way to grow. Let me say that again. What makes you comfortable can ruin you. And only in a state of discomfort can you continually grow. Now I suppose if on January 8th somebody come up and said, gee, buddy, getting fired is a good thing because now you're really going to grow. I probably would have smacked them. But pretty quickly I became motivated to start a new journey. And after a couple of years of work with my new team and a PhD friend at the University of Nebraska, we had this epiphany on how to illustrate and apply the science of discomfort and growth. But you have to learn to embrace it because it's the only environment where sustained or exponential growth can occur. So I use Claudette's actions not to heighten awareness of race issues, although that's not necessarily bad. But I use it as an example of every issue, of every situation in an ordered environment. It's a real and perfect example of complexity forcing people, our communities, and our courts into discomfort. And the downstream impact that can occur any time someone elects to move from order. Dr. Serene Jones, in a recent book, summarizes this concept very eloquently. She said, The constant facade of order hides the wilderness that is craving to seep out and teach us that life wasn't created to be what we think it is. Beyond words, we must experience the wilderness to be taught what cannot be otherwise known. So friends, it's not the discomfort of losing a job. It's not having a child breakdown on the tennis courts. But it's order you should fear the most because it is a threat. And order disrupting people like Jesus, Galileo, Claudette Colvin, Aspen's trainer, and maybe even a few of you have already proven. Now think about this. It's not the complexity triggering individuals or events you should fear the most. But it's your own willingness to accept or seek discomfort that will dictate the growth of not just you, but our entire world. Thank you very much for allowing me to be a part of this.
